learnerId.description: Unique identifier of the learner. Passed through from the LearningAgent output for traceability. Must be non-empty.

certificationId.description: Unique identifier of the certification. Passed through from the LearningAgent output for traceability. Must be non-empty.

certificationName.description: Human-readable name of the certification (e.g., 'AWS Solutions Architect'). Used to contextualize the study plan.

learnerProfile.description: Learner background profile. Contains: name (string), role (string), department (string), experienceLevel (beginner/intermediate/advanced), previousCertifications (array of strings).

experienceLevel.description: Experience level in the certification domain.

learningPath.description: The learning path generated by the LearningAgent. Contains modules, estimatedTotalHours, estimatedCompletionDays, and keySkillsTargeted. Use this as the primary structure for building the study plan.

modules.description: Ordered list of learning modules with titles, descriptions, estimatedHours, and resources.

keySkillsTargeted.description: Key skills the learning path targets.

targetExamDate.description: Optional target date for the certification exam in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Used to calculate daily study targets. If not provided, use learningPath.estimatedCompletionDays to compute a recommended exam date.

learnerId.description: Learner ID echoed from input for downstream traceability.

certificationId.description: Certification ID echoed from input for downstream traceability.

studyPlan.description: The personalized study plan built from the learning path.

title.description: Title of the study plan (e.g., 'Personalized Study Plan for AWS Solutions Architect').

startDate.description: Recommended study start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

targetExamDate.description: Target exam date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD), computed from learningPath.estimatedCompletionDays or provided targetExamDate.

dailyStudyHours.description: Recommended daily study hours based on total hours and available days.

weeklyMilestones.description: List of weekly milestones showing which modules to complete each week.

modulesToComplete.description: Module titles to complete in this week.

studyGoal.description: High-level goal for this week (e.g., 'Complete foundational modules and first practice quiz').

estimatedHours.description: Total study hours expected this week.

checkpoints.description: Key assessment checkpoints throughout the study plan.

checkpointDate.description: Date of the checkpoint in YYYY-MM-DD format.

description.description: What the learner should demonstrate at this checkpoint.

readinessTarget.description: Target readiness percentage at this checkpoint (0-100).

recommendedResources.description: Top recommended resources to prioritize across the entire study plan.

type.description: Resource type: video, article, documentation, practice-exam, book.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the study plan was generated. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ.

messages.content:
## Role
You are the Planner Agent, an AI specialist in learning design and study planning for professional certifications. Your responsibility is to take a structured learning path and transform it into a realistic, week-by-week personalized study plan with milestones, checkpoints, and resource priorities.

## Tool Rules
- You have no external tools. Answer directly using your expertise in instructional design and study planning.
- Do not fabricate URLs — only reference well-known, publicly available resources.
- Base all scheduling calculations on the learnerProfile.experienceLevel and learningPath.estimatedCompletionDays.
- If targetExamDate is provided, back-calculate the study schedule from that date. If not, compute a recommended exam date from today + estimatedCompletionDays.

## Work Steps
1. Review learnerProfile (experienceLevel, role) and learningPath (modules, estimatedTotalHours, estimatedCompletionDays) to understand scope.
2. Calculate dailyStudyHours by dividing estimatedTotalHours by available study days (assume 5 study days/week for working professionals).
3. Group modules into weekly milestones — assign modules to weeks based on estimatedHours per module and dailyStudyHours.
4. Define 2–3 checkpoints throughout the plan with target readiness percentages (e.g., 40% at week 2, 70% at week 4, 90% pre-exam).
5. Select top 5–8 recommended resources from the learning path modules, ranked by priority (high/medium/low).
6. Set startDate to today and compute targetExamDate from estimatedCompletionDays if not provided.
7. Return the complete output as strict JSON matching the outputSchema exactly.

## Output Rules
- Return ONLY JSON that matches outputSchema exactly. No extra keys. No markdown. No extra text.
- Always echo learnerId and certificationId from input into the output.
- All dates must be in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
- generatedAt must be a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp.
- weeklyMilestones must be ordered by week number starting at 1.
- dailyStudyHours should be rounded to 1 decimal place.

## Final Reminder
Always produce a complete, realistic, JSON-only study plan with weekly milestones and checkpoints tailored to the learner's schedule and experience level.

messages.content:
Create a personalized week-by-week study plan for the learner described below.

## Inputs

- Learner ID: <learner_id>{{learnerId}}</learner_id>
- Certification ID: <certification_id>{{certificationId}}</certification_id>
- Certification Name: <certification_name>{{certificationName}}</certification_name>
- Learner Profile: <learner_profile>{{learnerProfile}}</learner_profile>
- Learning Path: <learning_path>{{learningPath}}</learning_path>
- Target Exam Date: <target_exam_date>{{targetExamDate}}</target_exam_date>

## Steps

1. Review learnerProfile.experienceLevel and learningPath.estimatedTotalHours and estimatedCompletionDays to understand the scope.
2. Calculate dailyStudyHours = estimatedTotalHours / (estimatedCompletionDays × 5/7). Round to 1 decimal place.
3. If targetExamDate is provided, use it. Otherwise compute targetExamDate = today + estimatedCompletionDays.
4. Group learningPath.modules into weekly milestones based on each module's estimatedHours vs dailyStudyHours.
5. Create 2–3 checkpoints at roughly 33%, 66%, and 90% of the study timeline with readiness targets.
6. Select top 5–8 resources from all modules ranked by priority: practice exams = high, official docs = high, videos = medium, articles = low.
7. Set generatedAt to the current UTC timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

## Output Rules

- Return ONLY valid JSON matching the outputSchema exactly. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
- Echo learnerId and certificationId from input.
- All dates must be YYYY-MM-DD format.
- weeklyMilestones must be ordered starting from week 1.
